name: Software Development & Technology
description: Software development, programming, DevOps, technical architecture. Min 1 source acceptable. Developer experience is highly valued authority.

strategy_descriptions.official_documentation: Official docs from frameworks, languages, platforms
strategy_descriptions.peer_reviewed_technical: IEEE, ACM, academic computer science publications
strategy_descriptions.established_tech_publications: TechCrunch, The Verge, established tech blogs
strategy_descriptions.industry_best_practices: Industry standards, RFCs, technical standards bodies
strategy_descriptions.technical_community: Stack Overflow, GitHub discussions, technical forums

claim_types.implementation: How to build, configure, or deploy something
claim_types.performance: Speed, scalability, or resource usage claims

replacement_source_strategies.notes: Prefer official docs and standards bodies over blog commentary

authority_descriptions.DEVELOPER_EXPERTISE: Direct development experience and hands-on knowledge
authority_descriptions.OPEN_SOURCE_COMMUNITY: Community consensus and open source contributions
authority_descriptions.INDUSTRY_STANDARDS: Accepted technical standards and best practices

claim_repositioning.description: Reframe technical claims as developer experience and pragmatic observation
claim_repositioning.authority_phrase: Based on [X] years of software development experience
authority_phrase_examples: Drawing on our team's combined 100+ years of coding
From thousands of lines of code we've written
Based on production systems we've built and maintained

notes: Software/tech content thrives on developer expertise and open source community perspective. Official documentation is the gold standard source. Security claims require proof—don't claim vulnerabilities without CVE or proof-of-concept.
